questions.stem_en: What is the recommended minimum following distance under normal driving conditions?
questions.stem_zhHant: 正常駕駛條件下，建議的最小跟車距離是多少？
questions.explanation_en: Use the 3-second rule: maintain at least a 3-second following distance behind the vehicle ahead of you under normal conditions.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 使用3秒規則：正常情況下，與前車保持至少3秒的跟車距離。

questions.stem_en: When should you increase your following distance to 4 or more seconds?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼時候應該將跟車距離增加到4秒或更多？

options.text_en: In bad weather, heavy traffic, when following large vehicles, or on slippery roads
options.text_zhHant: 在惡劣天氣、擁堵交通、跟隨大型車輛或在濕滑路面上時

options.text_en: Only when driving in a school zone

questions.explanation_en: Increase your following distance to 4 or more seconds in bad weather, heavy traffic, when following large vehicles, or on slippery roads.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在惡劣天氣、擁堵交通、跟隨大型車輛或在濕滑路面上時，將跟車距離增加到4秒或更多。

questions.stem_en: When stopped behind another vehicle, how much space should you leave?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在另一輛車後面停車時，你應該留出多少空間？

options.text_en: Just enough so your bumpers almost touch
options.text_zhHant: 剛好讓保險槓幾乎接觸

options.text_en: Enough so you can see the rear tires of the vehicle ahead touching the pavement
options.text_zhHant: 足夠看到前車的後輪接觸地面

questions.explanation_en: When stopped behind another vehicle, leave enough space so you can see the rear tires of the vehicle in front of you touching the pavement.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在另一輛車後面停車時，留出足夠的空間以便能看到前車的後輪接觸地面。

questions.stem_en: Tailgating (following too closely) is a major cause of what type of collision?
questions.stem_zhHant: 緊跟前車（跟車太近）是哪種碰撞的主要原因？
questions.explanation_en: Tailgating (following too closely) is a major cause of rear-end collisions. It reduces your reaction time to stop safely.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 緊跟前車（跟車太近）是追尾事故的主要原因。它減少了你安全停車的反應時間。

questions.stem_en: If a driver is tailgating you, what should you do?
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果有人緊跟你，你應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Brake suddenly to teach them a lesson
options.text_zhHant: 急刹車給他們一個教訓

options.text_en: Gradually slow down to encourage them to pass, or change lanes
options.text_zhHant: 逐漸減速鼓勵他們超車，或者變道

questions.explanation_en: If a driver is tailgating you, gradually slow down to encourage them to pass, or change lanes to let them by. Do not brake suddenly.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果有人緊跟你，逐漸減速鼓勵他們超車，或者變道讓他們通過。不要急刹車。

questions.stem_en: Is it legal to hold and use a cell phone while driving in California?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在加州駕駛時手持使用手機是否合法？

options.text_en: Yes, as long as you are careful
No, it is illegal; you may use a hands-free device
options.text_zhHant: 不，這是違法的；你可以使用免提設備

options.text_en: Yes, but only for phone calls
Yes, but only at red lights

questions.explanation_en: It is illegal to hold and use a cell phone or electronic device while driving in California. You may use a hands-free device.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在加州駕駛時手持使用手機或電子設備是違法的。可以使用免提設備。

questions.stem_en: Can drivers under 18 use a hands-free device while driving?
questions.stem_zhHant: 18歲以下的駕駛員可以在駕駛時使用免提設備嗎？

options.text_en: Yes, hands-free is allowed for all drivers
options.text_zhHant: 是的，所有駕駛員都可以使用免提

options.text_en: No, they may not use any electronic device, even hands-free, except in emergencies
options.text_zhHant: 不，他們不得使用任何電子設備，包括免提設備，緊急情況除外

options.text_en: Yes, but only for GPS navigation

questions.explanation_en: Drivers under 18 may not use a cell phone or any electronic device while driving, even hands-free, except in emergencies.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 18歲以下的駕駛員駕駛時不得使用手機或任何電子設備，包括免提設備，緊急情況除外。

questions.stem_en: Approximately how long does sending or reading a text take your eyes off the road?
questions.stem_zhHant: 發送或閱讀短信大約會讓你的視線離開道路多長時間？
questions.explanation_en: Texting while driving is illegal and extremely dangerous. Sending or reading a text takes your eyes off the road for about 5 seconds.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 開車時發短信是違法且極其危險的。發送或閱讀短信會讓你的視線離開道路約5秒鐘。

questions.stem_en: If you use your phone for navigation while driving, how should it be set up?
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果你在駕駛時使用手機導航，應該如何設置？

options.text_en: Hold it in one hand while driving
Set it up before driving and mount it on the dashboard or windshield
options.text_zhHant: 在駕駛前設置好並安裝在儀表板或擋風玻璃上

options.text_en: Have a passenger hold it for you

questions.explanation_en: If you must use your phone for navigation, set it up before driving and mount it on the dashboard or windshield. You may only touch it with a single swipe or tap.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果需要使用手機導航，在駕駛前設置好並安裝在儀表板或擋風玻璃上。駕駛中只能單次滑動或點擊操作。

questions.stem_en: Which of the following is also considered distracted driving?
questions.stem_zhHant: 以下哪項也被視為分心駕駛？

options.text_en: Eating, grooming, or adjusting the radio
options.text_zhHant: 吃東西、化妝整理或調收音機

questions.explanation_en: Other forms of distracted driving include eating, grooming, reading, adjusting the radio, and interacting with passengers.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 其他分心駕駛行為包括吃東西、化妝整理、閱讀、調收音機和與乘客互動。

questions.stem_en: Who can be fined if a passenger under 16 is not wearing a seat belt?
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果16歲以下的乘客未系安全帶，誰會被罰款？

options.text_en: No one — seat belts are optional
options.text_zhHant: 沒有人——安全帶是可選的

questions.explanation_en: California law requires all vehicle occupants to wear seat belts. The driver can be fined for any unbelted passenger under 16.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 加州法律要求所有車內人員系安全帶。如果16歲以下的乘客未系安全帶，駕駛員會被罰款。

questions.stem_en: Where must children under 2 years old ride in a vehicle?
questions.stem_zhHant: 2歲以下的兒童必須坐在車輛的哪裡？

options.text_en: In the front seat with a seat belt
In a rear-facing car seat in the back seat
options.text_zhHant: 在後座的後向式安全座椅中

options.text_en: On an adult's lap in the back seat
In any car seat in any position
options.text_zhHant: 在任何位置的任何安全座椅中

questions.explanation_en: Children under 2 years old must ride in a rear-facing car seat in the back seat, unless they weigh 40 pounds or more, or are 40 inches or taller.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 2歲以下兒童必須坐在後座的後向式安全座椅中，除非體重達到40磅以上或身高達到40英寸以上。

questions.stem_en: Until what age must children be secured in a car seat or booster seat?
questions.stem_zhHant: 兒童到幾歲必須坐在安全座椅或增高座椅中？
questions.explanation_en: Children under 8 years old must be secured in a car seat or booster seat in the back seat of the vehicle.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 8歲以下的兒童必須坐在車輛後座的安全座椅或增高座椅中。

questions.stem_en: At what age or height can a child use a regular seat belt instead of a booster seat?
questions.stem_zhHant: 兒童到幾歲或多高可以使用普通安全帶代替增高座椅？

options.text_en: Age 8 or at least 4 feet 9 inches tall

questions.explanation_en: Children 8 years old or older, or who are at least 4 feet 9 inches tall, may use a regular seat belt.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 8歲或以上、或身高至少4英尺9英寸的兒童可以使用普通安全帶。

questions.stem_en: Why should you NEVER place a rear-facing car seat in front of an active airbag?
questions.stem_zhHant: 為什麼永遠不要將後向式安全座椅放在有激活安全氣囊的前座？

options.text_en: The airbag can injure or kill a child in a rear-facing seat
options.text_zhHant: 安全氣囊可能傷害或致死後向式座椅中的兒童

options.text_en: It is uncomfortable for the child
It is only a recommendation, not a law

questions.explanation_en: The back seat is the safest place for children 12 and under. Never place a rear-facing car seat in front of an active airbag because the airbag can seriously injure or kill the child.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 後座是12歲及以下兒童最安全的位置。不要將後向式安全座椅放在有激活安全氣囊的前座，因為安全氣囊可能嚴重傷害或致死兒童。

questions.stem_en: It is illegal to leave a child unattended in a vehicle if the child is:
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果兒童年齡為以下情況，將其無人看管地留在車內是違法的：
questions.explanation_en: It is illegal to leave a child who is 6 years old or younger unattended in a vehicle.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 將6歲或以下的兒童無人看管地留在車內是違法的。

questions.stem_en: How far ahead should you scan the road to anticipate hazards?
questions.stem_zhHant: 你應該掃視前方多遠的道路來預判危險？
questions.explanation_en: Scan the road ahead 10-15 seconds (about one block in the city, a quarter mile on the highway) to anticipate hazards.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 提前掃視前方10-15秒的道路（城市中約一個街區，高速公路上約四分之一英里），預判可能的危險。

questions.stem_en: How often should you check your mirrors while driving?
questions.stem_zhHant: 駕駛時應該多久查看一次後視鏡？

options.text_en: Only before turning or changing lanes

questions.explanation_en: Check your mirrors every 3-5 seconds to stay aware of traffic around you. Check your blind spot before changing lanes.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 每3-5秒查看後視鏡以了解周圍交通狀況。變道前檢查盲區。

questions.stem_en: If another driver is acting aggressively toward you, what should you do?
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果其他駕駛員對你有攻擊性行為，你應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Make eye contact and confront them
Speed up to get away from them
Do not make eye contact, do not challenge them, and do not block their path
options.text_zhHant: 不要與他們對視，不要挑釁他們，不要擋住他們的路

questions.explanation_en: If another driver is aggressive, do not make eye contact, do not challenge them, and do not block their path.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果其他駕駛員有攻擊性行為，不要與他們對視，不要挑釁他們，不要擋住他們的路。

questions.stem_en: What should you do if you feel drowsy while driving?
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果你在駕駛時感到困倦，應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Open the window for fresh air
options.text_zhHant: 打開車窗呼吸新鮮空氣

options.text_en: Pull over to a safe place and rest
options.text_zhHant: 靠邊停在安全的地方休息

options.text_en: Turn up the music to stay alert
options.text_zhHant: 把音樂調大以保持警覺

questions.explanation_en: Drowsy driving is as dangerous as drunk driving. If you are sleepy, pull over to a safe place and rest. Coffee or fresh air alone will not keep you alert.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 疲勞駕駛和醉駕一樣危險。如果感到困倦，靠邊停在安全的地方休息。僅靠咖啡或新鮮空氣不能保持清醒。

questions.stem_en: How far before turning should you signal in city areas?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在市區轉彎前應提前多遠打轉向燈？
questions.explanation_en: Signal at least 100 feet before turning in city areas and at least 200 feet before turning on highways.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在市區轉彎前至少提前100英尺打轉向燈，在高速公路上至少提前200英尺打轉向燈。

questions.stem_en: How far before turning should you signal on highways?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在高速公路上轉彎前應提前多遠打轉向燈？
questions.explanation_en: Signal at least 200 feet before turning on highways.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在高速公路上至少提前200英尺打轉向燈。

questions.stem_en: In the four zones of awareness around your vehicle, which zone is the 'Yellow' zone?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在車輛周圍的四個意識區域中，「黃色」區域是哪個？

options.text_en: The area ahead of your vehicle
The areas on both sides of your vehicle
Your blind spots — areas not visible in mirrors
options.text_zhHant: 你的盲區——後視鏡中看不到的區域

questions.explanation_en: The four zones of awareness are: Green (ahead), Blue (sides), Yellow (blind spots — areas not visible in mirrors), and Red (behind). The Yellow zone requires shoulder checks before changing lanes.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 四個意識區域為：綠色（前方）、藍色（兩側）、黃色（盲區——後視鏡中看不到的區域）和紅色（後方）。黃色區域需要在變道前轉頭檢查。

questions.stem_en: What is TRUE about carbon monoxide from vehicle exhaust?
questions.stem_zhHant: 關於車輛排放的一氧化碳，以下哪項是正確的？

options.text_en: It has a strong, noticeable smell
options.text_zhHant: 它有強烈的、明顯的氣味

options.text_en: It is odorless and colorless; never run your engine in a closed garage
options.text_zhHant: 它無色無味；永遠不要在封閉的車庫中啟動引擎

options.text_en: It is only dangerous to children
It is visible as blue smoke and easy to detect
options.text_zhHant: 它以藍色煙霧的形式可見，容易發現

questions.explanation_en: Carbon monoxide is an odorless, colorless, and deadly gas. Never start or run your engine in a closed garage, as carbon monoxide can build up and cause death.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 一氧化碳是一種無色、無味且致命的氣體。永遠不要在封閉的車庫中啟動或運行引擎，因為一氧化碳會積聚並導致死亡。

questions.stem_en: When dangers exist on both sides of the road, you should:
questions.stem_zhHant: 當道路兩側都存在危險時，你應該：

options.text_en: Speed up to get past the hazards quickly
options.text_zhHant: 加速快速通過危險區域

options.text_en: Give more space to the most dangerous situation
options.text_zhHant: 給最危險的情況留出更多空間

options.text_en: Stop immediately in the middle of the road
Close your eyes and drive straight

questions.explanation_en: When hazards are on both sides, give more space to the most dangerous situation.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 當兩側都有危險時，給最危險的情況留出更多空間。

questions.stem_en: When entering a freeway, what should you do on the on-ramp?
questions.stem_zhHant: 駛入高速公路時，在入口匝道上你應該做什麼？

options.text_en: Stop at the end of the ramp and wait for a gap
options.text_zhHant: 在匝道盡頭停車等待間隙

options.text_en: Accelerate to the speed of freeway traffic before merging
options.text_zhHant: 在匯入前加速到接近高速公路車流速度

options.text_en: Drive slowly and merge at 25 mph
options.text_zhHant: 慢速行駛並以25英里/小時匯入

options.text_en: Flash your lights to signal your intention

questions.explanation_en: When entering a freeway, use the on-ramp to accelerate to the speed of freeway traffic (or close to it) before merging.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 駛入高速公路時，利用入口匝道加速到接近高速公路車流速度後再匯入。

questions.stem_en: When exiting the freeway, when should you begin to slow down?
questions.stem_zhHant: 駛出高速公路時，什麼時候應該開始減速？

options.text_en: In the travel lanes before the exit
Only after you are in the exit lane or deceleration lane
options.text_zhHant: 只有在進入出口車道或減速車道之後

questions.explanation_en: When exiting the freeway, signal and move into the exit lane well in advance. Do not slow down until you are in the exit lane or deceleration lane.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 駛出高速公路時，提前打燈並駛入出口車道。在進入出口車道或減速車道之前不要減速。

questions.stem_en: If you miss your freeway exit, what should you do?
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果你錯過了高速公路出口，你應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Stop and back up to the exit
Cross the median to turn around

options.text_zhHant: 繼續行駛到下一個出口

options.text_en: Make a U-turn on the freeway

questions.explanation_en: If you miss your freeway exit, do not stop, back up, or try to cross the median. Continue to the next exit.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果錯過高速公路出口，不要停車、倒車或試圖穿越中央隔離帶。繼續行駛到下一個出口。

questions.stem_en: What is the minimum speed on most California freeways?
questions.stem_zhHant: 加州大多數高速公路的最低限速是多少？
questions.explanation_en: The minimum speed on most California freeways is 45 mph (unless posted otherwise or conditions require slower speeds).
questions.explanation_zhHant: 加州大多數高速公路的最低限速為45英里/小時（除非另有標示或路況需要降低速度）。

questions.stem_en: Should you stop on the freeway on-ramp before merging?
questions.stem_zhHant: 你應該在高速公路入口匝道上停車再匯入嗎？

options.text_en: Yes, always stop to check for traffic
options.text_zhHant: 是的，總是停車檢查車流

options.text_en: No, unless traffic is already stopped
Yes, stop and wait for a large gap

questions.explanation_en: Do not stop on the freeway on-ramp unless traffic is already stopped. Merge into traffic at or near the speed of traffic.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 除非車流已經停止，否則不要在高速公路入口匝道上停車。以接近車流速度匯入。

questions.stem_en: What free service does the California Highway Patrol Freeway Service Patrol (FSP) provide during commute times?
questions.stem_zhHant: 加州公路巡警高速公路服務巡邏隊（FSP）在通勤時段提供什麼免費服務？

options.text_en: A gallon of gas, jump starts, flat tire changes, and collision reports
options.text_zhHant: 一加侖汽油、啟動電瓶、更換漏氣輪胎和碰撞報告

questions.explanation_en: FSP provides free gas, jump starts, flat tire changes, and reports collisions to CHP. Call 511 for information.
questions.explanation_zhHant: FSP提供免費汽油、啟動電瓶、更換漏氣輪胎，並向CHP報告碰撞事故。撥打511了解信息。
